xanesnet.serialization package¶

Serialization package public API.

Submodules¶

xanesnet.serialization.auto_config module¶

Automatic model-configuration resolution from prepared datasets.

xanesnet.serialization.auto_config.resolve_auto_model_config(config, dataset)[source]¶

Resolve model fields set to "auto" from a prepared dataset.

The input config is expected to have passed training validation, including validation that "auto" is used only for supported top-level model fields. This function only performs the dataset-dependent finalization: it uses the registered batch processor for the dataset/model pair to prepare one sample, asks the model-specific resolver for concrete dimensions, and returns a new Config without mutating the input config.

Parameters:

  • config (Config) – Validated training configuration.

  • dataset (Dataset) – Prepared training dataset used to derive automatic model values.

Return type:

Config

Returns:

New configuration with requested automatic model fields replaced by concrete values.

Raises:

ConfigError – If automatic fields are requested for a model that has no resolver or if the resolver cann derive a requested value from the prepared sample.

xanesnet.serialization.checkpoints module¶

Checkpoint dataclass and helpers for XANESNET model persistence.

class xanesnet.serialization.checkpoints.Checkpoint(model_states, signature, optimizer_states=None, epochs=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Serializable snapshot of one or more trained models.

Parameters:

  • model_states (list[dict]) – List of model.state_dict() dicts, one per trained model.

  • signature (Config) – The Config used to produce this checkpoint.

  • optimizer_states (list[dict] | None) – Optional list of optimizer.state_dict() dicts, one per model.

  • epochs (list[int] | None) – Optional list of epoch counts, one per model.

model_states: list[dict]¶
signature: Config¶
optimizer_states: list[dict] | None = None¶
epochs: list[int] | None = None¶
save(path)[source]¶

Save the checkpoint to a .pth file.

Parameters:

path (str | Path) – Destination file path. Must have a .pth suffix.

Return type:

Path

Returns:

The resolved Path to the saved file.

Raises:

  • ValueError – If path does not end with .pth.

  • FileNotFoundError – If the parent directory does not exist.

__len__()[source]¶

Return the number of models stored in this checkpoint.

Return type:

int

Returns:

Number of serialized model state dictionaries.

classmethod load(path, map_location='cpu')[source]¶

Load a checkpoint from a .pth file.

Parameters:

  • path (str | Path) – Path to the .pth file.

  • map_location (str) – Device string passed to torch.load (default "cpu").

Return type:

Checkpoint

Returns:

A Checkpoint instance reconstructed from the saved state dict.

classmethod build(model_list, signature, optimizer_states=None, epochs=None)[source]¶

Build a Checkpoint from a list of trained models.

Parameters:

  • model_list (list[Model]) – Non-empty list of trained Model instances.

  • signature (Config) – The Config used for this training run.

  • optimizer_states (list[dict] | None) – Optional optimizer state dicts, one per model.

  • epochs (list[int] | None) – Optional epoch counts, one per model.

Return type:

Checkpoint

Returns:

A new Checkpoint containing the state dicts of all models.

Raises:

ValueError – If model_list is empty, or if optimizer_states or epochs are provided with lengths that do not match the number of models.

to_state_dict()[source]¶

Serialize this checkpoint to a plain dictionary suitable for torch.save.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Returns:

Dict with keys model_states, optimizer_states, epochs, and signature.

classmethod from_state_dict(state)[source]¶

Reconstruct a Checkpoint from a state dictionary.

Parameters:

state (dict[str, Any]) – Dictionary as produced by to_state_dict().

Return type:

Checkpoint

Returns:

A reconstructed Checkpoint instance.

__init__(model_states, signature, optimizer_states=None, epochs=None)¶

xanesnet.serialization.config module¶

Configuration loading, validation, and type-safe access for XANESNET.

xanesnet.serialization.config.load_raw_config(file_path)[source]¶

Load a YAML configuration file.

Parameters:

file_path (str | Path) – Path to the .yaml or .yml file to load.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Returns:

The parsed configuration as a ConfigRaw dictionary.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the file does not exist, is not a file, is not a valid YAML document, or does not contain a top-level mapping.

xanesnet.serialization.config.save_raw_config(config, file_path)[source]¶

Write a raw configuration dictionary to a YAML file.

Parameters:

  • config (dict[str, Any]) – The configuration data to serialize.

  • file_path (str | Path) – Destination path. Must end with .yaml or .yml.

Return type:

Path

Returns:

The resolved Path to the written file.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the extension is wrong, the parent directory does not exist, or the file already exists.

xanesnet.serialization.config.copy_raw_config(file_path, dst_dir, new_name=None)[source]¶

Copy a YAML configuration file to a destination directory.

Parameters:

  • file_path (str | Path) – Source file path.

  • dst_dir (str | Path) – Directory to copy the file into.

  • new_name (str | None) – Optional new filename (without directory). If None, the original filename is used.

Return type:

Path

Returns:

The Path to the copied file.

xanesnet.serialization.config.merge_raw_configs(a, b)[source]¶

Recursively merge two raw configuration dictionaries.

Parameters:

  • a (dict[str, Any]) – Base configuration dictionary.

  • b (dict[str, Any]) – Overlay configuration dictionary.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Returns:

A new ConfigRaw with all keys from both dicts. Nested dicts are merged recursively.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the same key exists in both dicts with conflicting non-dict values.

class xanesnet.serialization.config.Config(data)[source]¶

Bases: object

Validated, type-safe view over a raw YAML configuration.

Parameters:

data (dict[str, Any]) – A ConfigRaw dictionary (usually the result of load_raw_config).

__init__(data)[source]¶

Store a normalized raw configuration mapping.

section(section)[source]¶

Return a sub-section as a Config.

Parameters:

section (str) – Key of the sub-dictionary to wrap.

Return type:

Config

Returns:

A Config wrapping the section’s dictionary.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is missing or is not a dictionary.

optional_section(section)[source]¶

Return a sub-section as a Config, or None if absent.

Parameters:

section (str) – Key of the sub-dictionary to wrap.

Return type:

Config | None

Returns:

A Config wrapping the section’s dictionary, or None.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is present but is not a dictionary.

get_str(key)[source]¶

Return the value at key as a str.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

str

Returns:

The string value.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is missing or the value is not a str.

get_optional_str(key)[source]¶

Return the value at key as a str, or None if absent.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

str | None

Returns:

The string value, or None.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is present but the value is not a str.

get_int(key)[source]¶

Return the value at key as an int.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

int

Returns:

The integer value.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is missing or the value is not an int.

get_optional_int(key)[source]¶

Return the value at key as an int, or None if absent.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

int | None

Returns:

The integer value, or None.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is present but the value is not an int.

get_float(key)[source]¶

Return the value at key as a float.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

float

Returns:

The float value.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is missing or the value is not a float.

get_optional_float(key)[source]¶

Return the value at key as a float, or None if absent.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

float | None

Returns:

The float value, or None.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is present but the value is not a float.

get_bool(key)[source]¶

Return the value at key as a bool.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

The boolean value.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is missing or the value is not a bool.

get_optional_bool(key)[source]¶

Return the value at key as a bool, or None if absent.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

bool | None

Returns:

The boolean value, or None.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is present but the value is not a bool.

get_config_list(key)[source]¶

Return a list of Config objects from a list of dicts.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key that maps to a list of dicts.

Return type:

list[Config]

Returns:

List of Config objects, one per dict entry.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is missing, the value is not a list, or any element is not a dictionary.

get(key)[source]¶

Return the raw value for key.

Note

Not type-safe. Prefer the typed get_* accessors where possible.

Parameters:

key (str) – Config key to look up.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

The raw value associated with key.

Raises:

ConfigError – If the key is missing.

as_dict()[source]¶

Return a deep copy of the underlying raw config dictionary.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Returns:

A ConfigRaw deep-copy of the internal data.

as_kwargs()[source]¶

Convert the config into keyword arguments suitable for a class or function call.

Nested dicts are wrapped in Config; lists of dicts become lists of Config; all other values are returned as deep copies.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Returns:

Dict mapping config keys to converted values.

save(file_path)[source]¶

Save this config to a YAML file.

Parameters:

file_path (str | Path) – Destination path. Must end with .yaml or .yml.

Return type:

Path

Returns:

The resolved Path to the written file.

Raises:

ConfigError – Propagated from save_raw_config.

update(other)[source]¶

Merge another Config into this one.

Parameters:

other (Config) – The Config whose values are overlaid onto this one.

Raises:

ConfigError – If any key has conflicting non-dict values.

Return type:

None

update_with_dict(other)[source]¶

Merge a raw dictionary into this config.

Parameters:

other (dict[str, Any]) – Raw config dictionary whose values are overlaid onto this one.

Raises:

ConfigError – If any key has conflicting non-dict values.

Return type:

None

xanesnet.serialization.config.validate_config_train(config)[source]¶

Validate a config dict for a training run.

Training configs may set selected top-level model fields to "auto". Those values are validated here and resolved later, after the dataset has been prepared.

Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any]) – Raw configuration dictionary.

Return type:

Config

Returns:

A validated Config object.

Raises:

ConfigError – If "auto" is used for an unsupported model field.

xanesnet.serialization.config.validate_config_infer(config)[source]¶

Validate a config dict for an inference run.

Inference model architecture is restored from the checkpoint signature, so model fields set to "auto" are rejected instead of being re-detected from the inference dataset.

Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any]) – Raw configuration dictionary.

Return type:

Config

Returns:

A validated Config object.

Raises:

ConfigError – If any model value is set to "auto".

xanesnet.serialization.config.validate_config_analyze(config)[source]¶

Validate a config dict for an analysis run.

Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any]) – Raw configuration dictionary.

Return type:

Config

Returns:

A Config object (minimal validation applied).

xanesnet.serialization.config.validate_config(config)[source]¶

Run full config validation and fill in defaults.

Parameters:

config (dict[str, Any]) – Raw configuration dictionary to validate and mutate in-place.

Return type:

Config

Returns:

A Config object wrapping the validated and defaulted data.

Raises:

ConfigError – If any required key is missing, a type key is absent, or mutually exclusive sections coexist.

xanesnet.serialization.defaults module¶

Default configuration values and required-key specifications for all XANESNET components.

xanesnet.serialization.jsonl_stream module¶

Lazy JSONL stream reader and JSON serialization helpers for XANESNET.

class xanesnet.serialization.jsonl_stream.JSONLStream(path, count=None)[source]¶

Bases: Iterable[dict[str, Any]]

Lazy iterable that reads JSONL lines from a file on demand.

Parameters:

  • path (Path) – Path to the .jsonl file to read.

  • count (int | None) – Pre-known number of lines. When None, the count is resolved lazily from a .meta.json sidecar or by scanning the file.

__init__(path, count=None)[source]¶

Initialize a lazy JSONL reader.

__iter__()[source]¶

Yield each non-empty line of the JSONL file as a parsed dict.

Return type:

Iterator[dict[str, Any]]

Returns:

Iterator over decoded JSON objects, one per non-empty line.

__len__()[source]¶

Return the number of records in the stream.

If a count was not provided at construction time, the value is resolved from a .meta.json sidecar file (key count). If no sidecar exists, all lines are counted by scanning the file.

Return type:

int

Returns:

Total number of non-empty lines / records.

xanesnet.serialization.jsonl_stream.json_friendly(value)[source]¶

Convert a value to a JSON-serializable form.

Conversion priority: 1. None and JSON primitives (str, int, float, bool) are

returned as-is.

  1. Path objects are converted to strings.

  2. Dicts and lists/tuples are recursed into.

  3. Objects with an .item() method (e.g. numpy scalars) are unwrapped.

  4. Objects with a .tolist() method (e.g. numpy/torch arrays) are converted to nested Python lists.

  5. Anything else is coerced to str.

Parameters:

value (Any) – Any Python value to make JSON-friendly.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

A JSON-serializable Python object.

xanesnet.serialization.models module¶

Utilities for saving and loading XANESNET model weights.

xanesnet.serialization.models.save_model(dst_dir, model)[source]¶

Save a single model’s weights to model_weights.pth inside dst_dir.

Parameters:

  • dst_dir (str | Path) – Directory in which to write the weights file. Created if it does not already exist.

  • model (Model) – The model whose state_dict() will be saved.

Return type:

None

xanesnet.serialization.models.save_models(dst_dir, model_list)[source]¶

Save one or more models to dst_dir.

If a single model is provided, its weights are written directly to dst_dir/model_weights.pth. For multiple models, each is saved in its own numbered sub-directory (model_0/, model_1/, …).

Parameters:

  • dst_dir (str | Path) – Destination directory.

  • model_list (list[Model]) – Non-empty list of models to save.

Raises:

ValueError – If model_list is empty.

Return type:

None

xanesnet.serialization.models.load_pretrained_model()[source]¶

Load a pre-trained model.

Note

Not implemented yet.

Return type:

Any

Returns:

Loaded model once implemented.

Raises:

NotImplementedError – Always.

xanesnet.serialization.prediction_readers module¶

Prediction reader classes for XANESNET inference outputs.

class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_readers.PredictionSample[source]¶

Bases: TypedDict

Single-absorber prediction record returned by PredictionReader.

All values are numpy arrays or torch tensors with no leading absorber dimension - each field corresponds to a single row of the equivalent PredictionBatch field.

prediction: ndarray | Tensor¶
target: ndarray | Tensor¶
file_name: NotRequired[str]¶
forward_time: NotRequired[float]¶
forward_time_pass: NotRequired[float]¶
class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_readers.PredictionReader(path)[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for reading saved XANESNET inference results.

Implements the Iterator protocol so that callers can iterate over predictions one absorber at a time.

Parameters:

path (str | Path) – Directory (or file) containing the saved prediction data.

__init__(path)[source]¶

Initialize a prediction reader for a saved predictions location.

abstractmethod __len__()[source]¶

Return the total number of absorber records in the dataset.

Return type:

int

abstractmethod __getitem__(index)[source]¶

Return the PredictionSample at position index.

Parameters:

index (int) – Zero-based absorber index.

Return type:

PredictionSample

Returns:

A PredictionSample for the requested absorber.

__iter__()[source]¶

Reset the iteration cursor and return self as the iterator.

Return type:

Iterator[PredictionSample]

Returns:

This reader instance, positioned at the first sample.

__next__()[source]¶

Return the next PredictionSample and advance the cursor.

Return type:

PredictionSample

Returns:

The next absorber record.

Raises:

StopIteration – When all absorbers have been yielded.

get_all()[source]¶

Load all predictions at once.

Return type:

PredictionBatch

Returns:

A PredictionBatch with all absorbers stacked along the leading absorber dimension.

close()[source]¶

Close any open resources. No-op by default; override in subclasses.

Return type:

None

__enter__()[source]¶

Enter the context manager and return self.

Return type:

PredictionReader

Returns:

This reader instance.

__exit__(exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb)[source]¶

Exit the context manager and close open resources.

Parameters:

  • exc_type (type[BaseException] | None) – Exception type raised inside the context, if any.

  • exc_val (BaseException | None) – Exception instance raised inside the context, if any.

  • exc_tb (object) – Traceback associated with the exception, if any.

Return type:

None

class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_readers.HDF5Reader(path)[source]¶

Bases: PredictionReader

Prediction reader backed by an HDF5 file written by HDF5Writer.

Parameters:

path (str | Path) – Directory containing the predictions.h5 file.

__init__(path)[source]¶

Open an HDF5-backed predictions directory.

__len__()[source]¶

Return the number of absorber records stored in the HDF5 file.

Return type:

int

Returns:

Number of persisted absorber rows.

__getitem__(index)[source]¶

Return a single absorber record from the HDF5 dataset.

Parameters:

index (int) – Zero-based absorber index.

Return type:

PredictionSample

Returns:

Decoded prediction sample for index.

get_all()[source]¶

Load every HDF5 dataset into a single stacked prediction batch.

Return type:

PredictionBatch

Returns:

Mapping from field names to full absorber-major numpy arrays.

close()[source]¶

Close the underlying HDF5 file handle if it is still open.

Return type:

None

class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_readers.NumpyReader(path)[source]¶

Bases: PredictionReader

Prediction reader for .npz files written by NumpyWriter.

Expects files named sample_XXXXXX.npz in the given directory.

Parameters:

path (str | Path) – Directory containing the sample_*.npz files.

__init__(path)[source]¶

Index a directory of per-sample .npz prediction files.

__len__()[source]¶

Return the number of discovered .npz sample files.

Return type:

int

Returns:

Number of readable prediction samples.

__getitem__(index)[source]¶

Load a single prediction sample from disk.

Parameters:

index (int) – Zero-based absorber index.

Return type:

PredictionSample

Returns:

Decoded prediction sample for index.

class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_readers.JSONReader(path)[source]¶

Bases: PredictionReader

Prediction reader for .json files written by JSONWriter.

Expects files named sample_XXXXXX.json in the given directory.

Parameters:

path (str | Path) – Directory containing the sample_*.json files.

__init__(path)[source]¶

Index a directory of per-sample JSON prediction files.

__len__()[source]¶

Return the number of discovered JSON sample files.

Return type:

int

Returns:

Number of readable prediction samples.

__getitem__(index)[source]¶

Load a single prediction sample from a JSON file.

Parameters:

index (int) – Zero-based absorber index.

Return type:

PredictionSample

Returns:

Decoded prediction sample for index.

xanesnet.serialization.prediction_readers.detect_prediction_format(path)[source]¶

Infer the prediction format from directory contents.

Parameters:

path (str | Path) – Path to the predictions directory.

Return type:

type[PredictionReader]

Returns:

The appropriate PredictionReader subclass (HDF5Reader, NumpyReader, or JSONReader).

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If path does not exist.

  • ValueError – If no recognisable prediction files are found.

xanesnet.serialization.prediction_writers module¶

Prediction writer classes for XANESNET inference outputs.

class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_writers.PredictionBatch[source]¶

Bases: TypedDict

Batch of per-absorber predictions ready to be persisted.

All values are numpy arrays or torch tensors whose first dimension is the absorber dimension - every field carries one row per absorber site. All array-like fields must share the same leading absorber size.

prediction: ndarray | Tensor¶
target: ndarray | Tensor¶
file_name: NotRequired[ndarray]¶
forward_time: NotRequired[ndarray | Tensor]¶
forward_time_pass: NotRequired[ndarray | Tensor]¶
class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_writers.PredictionWriter(path, buffer_size)[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for writing XANESNET inference results to disk.

Buffers incoming PredictionBatch objects and flushes them in batches to keep memory usage bounded. All storage is indexed along the absorber dimension.

Parameters:

  • path (str | Path) – Directory where output data will be written.

  • buffer_size (int) – Number of absorber rows to accumulate before flushing to storage.

__init__(path, buffer_size)[source]¶

Initialize a prediction writer and its on-disk storage.

add(batch)[source]¶

Buffer a batch of absorber predictions.

The batch is accumulated in an internal buffer. When the buffer reaches buffer_size rows, it is automatically flushed to storage.

Parameters:

batch (PredictionBatch) – A PredictionBatch where every field’s first dimension is the absorber dimension. All fields must have the same leading absorber size.

Raises:

  • ValueError – If any value has no leading absorber dimension, the absorber sizes across fields are inconsistent, the set of fields differs from earlier batches, or the batch is empty.

  • TypeError – If a boolean or string array has more than one dimension per absorber.

Return type:

None

flush()[source]¶

Flush all buffered data to storage.

Concatenates buffered chunks for each field and delegates to _write_batch. Clears the buffer afterwards.

Return type:

None

close()[source]¶

Flush any remaining buffered data and close storage resources.

Return type:

None

class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_writers.HDF5Writer(path, buffer_size=100000, compression='gzip')[source]¶

Bases: PredictionWriter

HDF5-backed prediction writer.

Appends absorber rows to datasets inside a single predictions.h5 file.

Supported per-absorber payload types: - Numeric arrays of any shape. - Per-absorber scalar float, int, or bool values. - Per-absorber scalar str / bytes values.

Bool and string arrays (ndim > 0 per absorber) are not supported.

Parameters:

  • path (str | Path) – Directory in which predictions.h5 will be created.

  • buffer_size (int) – Number of absorber rows to buffer before flushing.

  • compression (str) – HDF5 compression filter name (default "gzip").

__init__(path, buffer_size=100000, compression='gzip')[source]¶

Initialize an HDF5-backed prediction writer.

class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_writers.NumpyWriter(path, buffer_size)[source]¶

Bases: PredictionWriter

Prediction writer that saves one .npz file per absorber.

Each file is named sample_XXXXXX.npz and contains all fields for that absorber. Useful for debugging or small datasets.

class xanesnet.serialization.prediction_writers.JSONWriter(path, buffer_size)[source]¶

Bases: PredictionWriter

Prediction writer that saves one .json file per absorber.

Each file is named sample_XXXXXX.json and contains all fields for that absorber as human-readable JSON. Useful for debugging or small datasets.

xanesnet.serialization.splits module¶

Dataset split index loading and saving for XANESNET.

xanesnet.serialization.splits.load_split_indices(filepath)[source]¶

Load dataset split indices from a JSON file.

The file must have an indices dict at the top level. Keys can be the string literals "train" and "valid" (mapped to positions 0 and 1 respectively) or integer strings.

Parameters:

filepath (str | Path) – Path to the JSON split-index file.

Return type:

list[list[int]]

Returns:

An ordered list[list[int]] where each inner list contains the dataset indices for that split position.

Raises:

ResourceError – If the file format is invalid, required keys are missing, duplicate or conflicting key definitions are present, or any split’s indices are not a list of integers.

xanesnet.serialization.splits.save_split_indices(filepath, split_indices_list, train_valid_keys=True)[source]¶

Save dataset split indices to a JSON file.

Parameters:

  • filepath (str | Path) – Destination path for the JSON file. Parent directories are created automatically.

  • split_indices_list (list[list[int]]) – Ordered list of index lists, one per split.

  • train_valid_keys (bool) – If True (default), positions 0 and 1 are written with the human-readable keys "train" and "valid" instead of "0" and "1".

Return type:

None

xanesnet.serialization.tensorboard module¶

Singleton TensorBoard logger for XANESNET training runs.

class xanesnet.serialization.tensorboard.TensorBoardLogger[source]¶

Bases: object

Singleton TensorBoard logger for XANESNET training runs.

Use new_run to initialize logging for each new training run. All log_* methods silently no-op when the logger has not been initialized or when TensorBoard has been disabled.

Note

Instantiate via tb_logger = TensorBoardLogger() and import the module-level singleton rather than creating fresh instances.

static __new__(cls)[source]¶

Create or return the shared TensorBoard writer.

Return type:

TensorBoardLogger

property enabled: bool¶

Whether TensorBoard logging is currently active for this process.

set_config(config)[source]¶

Attach the run configuration used for later hyperparameter logging.

Parameters:

config (Config) – Validated configuration for the upcoming run.

Return type:

None

new_run(save_dir)[source]¶

Initialize a new TensorBoard run.

Closes any previously open writer, creates a new SummaryWriter pointing at save_dir, and logs a hyperparameter table if a config has been set via set_config.

Parameters:

save_dir (str | Path) – Directory in which TensorBoard event files will be written.

Return type:

None

close()[source]¶

Flush pending events and close the underlying SummaryWriter.

Return type:

None

property writer: SummaryWriter¶

Return the active SummaryWriter.

Returns:

The current SummaryWriter instance.

Raises:

RuntimeError – If new_run() has not been called.

log_epoch_metrics(epoch, train_loss, train_regularization, train_total, valid_loss=None, valid_regularization=None, valid_total=None)[source]¶

Log per-epoch training and optional validation metrics.

Parameters:

  • epoch (int) – Current epoch index (used as the global step).

  • train_loss (float) – Training data loss.

  • train_regularization (float) – Training regularization term.

  • train_total (float) – Total training loss (loss + regularization).

  • valid_loss (float | None) – Validation data loss (None to skip).

  • valid_regularization (float | None) – Validation regularization term (None to skip).

  • valid_total (float | None) – Total validation loss (None to skip).

Return type:

None

log_learning_rate(epoch, lr)[source]¶

Log the current learning rate at the given epoch.

Parameters:

  • epoch (int) – Current epoch index.

  • lr (float) – Learning rate value.

Return type:

None

log_model_weights(epoch, model)[source]¶

Log histograms of model parameter values and gradients.

Parameters:

  • epoch (int) – Current epoch index (used as the global step).

  • model (Module) – The model whose parameters are logged.

Return type:

None

log_model_graph(model, input_example)[source]¶

Log the model computation graph (call once at the start of training).

Inputs are ordered according to the model’s forward signature. Non-tensor inputs are bound automatically using _TensorBoardGraphWrapper. Failures during tracing are caught and logged as warnings rather than propagated.

Parameters:

  • model (Module) – The model to trace.

  • input_example (dict[str, Any]) – Dict mapping forward-argument names to example values.

Return type:

None

log_scalar(tag, value, step)[source]¶

Log a single scalar value.

Parameters:

  • tag (str) – TensorBoard tag (used as the series name).

  • value (float) – Scalar value to record.

  • step (int) – Global step / epoch index.

Return type:

None

log_histogram(tag, values, step)[source]¶

Log a histogram of tensor values.

Parameters:

  • tag (str) – TensorBoard tag.

  • values (Tensor) – Tensor whose distribution is recorded.

  • step (int) – Global step / epoch index.

Return type:

None

log_text(tag, text, step)[source]¶

Log a text string.

Parameters:

  • tag (str) – TensorBoard tag.

  • text (str) – The text content to record.

  • step (int) – Global step / epoch index.

Return type:

None

log_figure(tag, figure, step)[source]¶

Log a matplotlib figure as an image.

Parameters:

  • tag (str) – TensorBoard tag.

  • figure (Any) – A matplotlib.figure.Figure instance.

  • step (int) – Global step / epoch index.

Return type:

None

log_final_metrics(metric_dict)[source]¶

Log final run-level metrics alongside hyperparameters.

Uses add_hparams so that hyperparameters and final metrics appear in the TensorBoard HPARAMS tab. Call once at the end of training.

Parameters:

metric_dict (dict[str, float]) – Mapping from metric name to final scalar value.

Return type:

None

flush()[source]¶

Flush pending events to disk.

Return type:

None
